title: Autopilot, Intune & SCCM Endpoint Standardization
summary: Standardized endpoint provisioning on Microsoft Autopilot — new hardware ships pre-configured, compliant, and enrolled with no IT touch. Intune MDM/MAM for modern devices, SCCM co-management for legacy. Compliance reporting unified across the entire fleet.
problem: Endpoint provisioning was manual, inconsistent, and slow. Every new device went through a hands-on imaging process that took hours and produced configurations that drifted between technicians. Legacy devices ran SCCM, modern devices needed Intune, and the two never reported into the same dashboard.
approach: Negotiated direct vendor-to-Autopilot device registration so hardware ships from the OEM already associated with the tenant
Built deployment profiles for each user persona — clinical, admin, field — with role-appropriate apps, policies, and Conditional Access posture
Configured Defender for Endpoint as the compliance signal source so device health drives Conditional Access decisions
Enabled SCCM co-management for the legacy Windows estate so policy and patching flow from a single MEM admin center
Documented and trained the help desk on the zero-touch flow so escalations stay rare
lessons: Autopilot only delivers value if the OEM relationship is wired in correctly — devices that arrive in the wrong tenant or unregistered turn the zero-touch flow into a manual recovery exercise. Spend the time with the hardware vendor to get the registration pipeline right and audit it monthly. Also: persona-based deployment profiles age better than device-class profiles, because hardware refreshes but the persona stays the same.
